description_long: This is a large, open lily flower with six broad, slightly ruffled petals that are pale pink with subtle yellow and cream undertones, and faint speckling near the center. The petals are arranged in a radial pattern, curving outward and slightly downward, giving the flower a dramatic, star-like appearance. Prominent dark brown stamens with elongated anthers and a single central pistil extend upward from the flower's throat. The flower is attached to a long, slender green stem, which is visible and upright. A single, lanceolate green leaf is present near the base, detached in some views. The overall appearance is delicate and organic, with soft transitions between colors and a natural, slightly wilted texture.
description: A large, pale pink lily flower with six broad, ruffled petals, prominent stamens, and a central pistil on a slender green stem. The flower has a natural, delicate appearance with a single green leaf nearby.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open face of the lily, with all six petals radiating outward and the central stamens and pistil clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the upward extension of the pistil and stamens, with the petals curving outward and a detached green leaf visible below.
description_view_2: Rear oblique view showing the back of the petals and the base of the flower, with the stem and a single leaf visible.
description_view_3: Back view displaying the reverse side of the petals, the stem, and the detached leaf, with less visibility of the flower's reproductive structures.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, with six distinct petals radiating symmetrically from the center. The petals overlap slightly at their bases and curve outward, creating a star-like, open structure typical of many lilies.
